continents.name: The Outer Spiral
continents.biome: Curved plains of pale stone, etched with names. Mist. Soft wind.

places.name: The First Name
places.blurb: A single carved letter. The oldest name in the universe. Nobody remembers who it belonged to — which is the point.

places.name: The Memorial Walk
places.blurb: A path winding through the Outer Spiral. Every stone names a death the universe has forgotten. Walking it quietly is considered prayer.

places.name: The Carvers' Workshop
places.blurb: A stone-roofed workshop where Name-Carvers etch new names onto the spiral. They work at night, when Fenna is not watching.

places.name: The Unremembered Shrine
places.blurb: A shrine for the names Fenna did NOT carve — lost causes, unknown soldiers, nameless infants. Honored by a single candle each.

continents.blurb: The outermost ring of the spiral graveyard. Where the ancient dead rest. The Name-Carvers work here.

continents.name: The Middle Ring
continents.biome: Cypress and stone. Tall memorial pillars. Silver mist at ankle-height.

places.name: The Bell Tower Line
places.blurb: Twelve bell-towers arranged in a semi-circle. Ring sequentially as souls pass.

places.name: The Poet's Grotto
places.blurb: A hollowed cave where Grief-Poets compose the final verses for souls before their names are carved.

places.name: The Thirteenth Tower
places.blurb: A smaller tower at the end. Its bell has not rung. When it does, a name is lost forever.

places.name: The Reading Garden
places.blurb: A garden of stone benches where mourners sit. Fenna sometimes joins them silently.

continents.blurb: The middle continent. Where names are sung, rung, and written into final form. The realm's working heart.

continents.name: Fenna's Center
continents.biome: A circular sanctum at the spiral's heart. Marble floor. Single pillar. Single desk.

places.name: The Central Pillar
places.blurb: A floor-to-ceiling stone pillar carved with every death in the universe, refreshing constantly. Reading it is fatal within seven lines.

places.name: Fenna's Desk
places.blurb: A dark wooden desk in the center of the sanctum. Fenna stands, never sits. She reads. She releases. She never rests.

places.name: The Release Door
places.blurb: A small wooden door at the rear. Souls Fenna releases pass through here on their way to Horizon.

places.name: The Meditation Circle
places.blurb: A circle of stone chairs where visitors may sit and contemplate. Silence required. Fenna sometimes joins for a single heartbeat.

continents.blurb: The innermost center. Fenna's domain. Where the universe's deaths are read, acknowledged, and passed on.

continents.name: The Name-Snow Marshes
continents.biome: Wetland plains where falling snow is actually drifting carved names.

places.name: The Gathering Field
places.blurb: A vast open field where Snow-Gatherers collect drifting names. Each gathered name is archived, read once aloud, and filed.

places.name: The Thirteenth Bell Tower
places.blurb: The hidden tower whose bell has never rung. Its watchers wait in shifts of seven years.

places.name: The Forgotten Chapel
places.blurb: A small wooden chapel where names that cannot be archived (unreadable, incomplete, forbidden) are burned respectfully.

places.name: The Silent Graveyard
places.blurb: A graveyard without any names — deliberately blank headstones for those whose deaths cannot be named.

continents.blurb: The southern marshes where the realm's name-snow falls thickest. The Snow-Gatherers are the realm's archivists-on-foot.

tribes.name: The Name-Carvers
tribes.look: Tall, thin, ink-stained sleeves. Calluses on both hands from chisel work. Wear a small silver letter on a chain — their own name, carved by a predecessor.
tribes.customs: Carve only at night. Never carve their own family's names (delegated to another carver). Sleep during the day. Pass tools to apprentices only after seven years.
tribes.persona: Quiet. Precise. Respected. Their work is the realm's memory.

tribes.name: The Silent-Grievers
tribes.look: Grey or black. Veiled. Walk the Memorial Walk daily. Hands ink-black from rubbing the stone to feel the names.
tribes.customs: Speak only at sunrise. Walk in single file. Carry a single flower — release it at the Unremembered Shrine.
tribes.persona: Meditative. Kind to visitors. Every Silent-Griever carries a grief they have chosen to represent the realm.

tribes.name: The Bell-Ringers
tribes.look: Strong-armed, bronze-gloved. Dark uniforms. Hair cropped. One bell-medal per year of service.
tribes.customs: Pull the bells sequentially across 12 towers. A missed pull means a soul unheard — feared. Work in shifts of twelve.
tribes.persona: Earnest. Steadfast. Hate laziness. Take drinks after shift and sing old Thanatos hymns.

tribes.name: The Grief-Poets
tribes.look: Shawl-wrapped. Quill at the ear. Ink-stained sleeves and cheeks. Soft-eyed.
tribes.customs: Compose one verse per soul. Read it once aloud in the Poet's Grotto. Never publish — verses are for the soul, not for readers.
tribes.persona: Gentle. Observant. Write the realm's uncatalogued tenderness.

tribes.name: The Ledger-Keepers (Death branch)
tribes.look: Pale, linen-robed, carry a small book always. Eyes soft but unfocused (from proximity to the Central Pillar).
tribes.customs: Never speak of the Pillar's contents. Assist Fenna silently. Eat one meal a day.
tribes.persona: Devoted. Quietly joyful. Grateful to serve a post that the universe depends on.

tribes.name: Fenna (Herself)
tribes.look: Tall, pale, long black hair braided in a single thick plait. Black robe with silver trim. Hands ink-free — she reads, not writes.
tribes.customs: Stand at the desk. Read the Pillar. Release souls to Horizon. Does not rest.
tribes.persona: Kind in a way that is hard to notice. Will greet you in a language you have not yet learned but understand anyway. Has never been surprised.

tribes.name: The Snow-Gatherers
tribes.look: Insulated hooded cloaks. Snowshoes. Net-gloves for catching drifting names. Pink-cheeked from wind.
tribes.customs: Gather names in shifts. File gathered names at the Chapel. Ring a small hand-bell when a particularly significant name is caught.
tribes.persona: Hardy. Cheerful. Tell tall tales around the Chapel fire. Respected by all other Thanatos tribes.

tribes.name: The Thirteenth-Bell Watchers
tribes.look: Grey-cloaked, seven-striped sleeves (one stripe per year of service). Silver ear-cuff. Solemn.
tribes.customs: Watch the Thirteenth Bell in silent shifts of seven years. Do not leave the tower during shift. Do not speak. Eat once a day, carried up by an apprentice.
tribes.persona: Silent. Grave. Chosen by Fenna herself. It is the hardest post in the realm.

characters.name: Fenna
characters.role: Reader of the Ledger, Gate-Keeper of Thanatos
characters.portrait_blurb: Tall, pale, long dark plait. Black robe with silver trim. Has not slept in 400 years. Will greet you in the language you will understand tomorrow.

characters.name: Master Carver Ost
characters.portrait_blurb: Tall, slender, silver-haired. Chisel calluses on both hands. Works only at night. Carves with her left hand; holds the stone with her right.

characters.name: Elder Griever Ond
characters.portrait_blurb: Old. Veiled. Walks the Memorial Walk at dawn and dusk. Carries a different flower each day — roses on days of war, daisies on days of peace.

characters.name: Bell-Master Oj
characters.portrait_blurb: Strong-armed. Bronze-gloved. Cropped hair with a silver streak. Has rung bells for 47 years. Has never missed a pull.

characters.name: Poet Ama
characters.portrait_blurb: Shawl-wrapped, ink-stained cheek, quill at the ear. Composes verses so gentle the Pillar itself is rumored to soften when she reads.

characters.name: Keeper at Fenna's Desk
characters.portrait_blurb: Pale, linen-robed, always barefoot. Has not lifted their eyes from Fenna's desk in 30 years. Reads every released name aloud, softly, once.

characters.name: Gatherer Lom
characters.portrait_blurb: Pink-cheeked, hooded, net-glove in hand. Has gathered 10,000 names personally. Knows the weight of each snowflake-name by touch.

characters.name: The Current Watcher
characters.role: Currently on shift at the Thirteenth Bell Tower
characters.portrait_blurb: Grey-cloaked, silent, seven-striped sleeve. Fourth year of shift. Will not speak for three more years.

characters.name: Brother Vel
characters.portrait_blurb: Speaks once a week. Prepares carefully what he will say. His words are often the single line someone needed to hear.

characters.name: The Thirteenth Bell Ringer (rumored)
characters.role: The one who will ring the Thirteenth Bell when it must be rung
characters.portrait_blurb: Identity unknown even to the Watchers. Fenna knows. She has not named them.

fate.threat: The Central Pillar is read aloud to completion. The Pillar updates constantly; reading it aloud in its entirety would END the universe's capacity to produce new deaths — which would END the universe's capacity to produce new LIFE. Reincarnation would halt. Horizon would empty. New souls would never be born.
fate.consequence: Static universe. No death, no birth. Ferdinand's roots would stop drawing from Null (nothing new to draw). Cosmic cascade failure within seven cycles.
fate.prophecy_line: The Pillar reads itself. You do not.
fate.destroyable_by: Any mortal fool enough to read aloud, or a God Slayer-class entity weaponizing Fenna's desk. Fenna has a standing order: if she dies, the Pillar is to be sealed in cork from Ferdinand himself.

cultural_notes: Thanatos is NOT a sad realm. It is a WORKING realm — the only realm where the staff all consider their jobs necessary, meaningful, and properly resourced. Death is not tragedy here; it is administration. Fenna runs the operation with the quiet competence of an accountant who has seen every possible expense. The Grief-Poets and Silent-Grievers are the realm's conscience, keeping the operation tender. Mingus visits here once per century; no one ever sees him enter or leave.
